title: sedum ochroleucum subsp. montanum
intro: Sedum ochroleucum subsp. montanum, also known as Yellow-leaved Mountain Stonecrop, is a subspecies of succulent plants belonging to the family Crassulaceae. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sedum ochroleucum subsp. montanum include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems.
